scope: Second-pass audit under the 'trace every claimed failure-mode guarantee to executable code' methodology. Pass-1 line numbers were mass-stale after the R6 Wave 2 split that shrank lib.rs from 1400+ LoC to 142 LoC across 11 modules (builder.rs, change.rs, change_probe.rs, engine.rs, engine_transaction.rs, error.rs, outcome.rs, primitive_host.rs, subgraph_spec.rs, testing.rs). Every pass-1 disposition was re-walked against current code; one new real finding (PrimitiveHost edge replay silently drops buffered ops, breaking the atomicity claim for PrimitiveHost::put_edge / delete_edge) was surfaced that pass-1 missed because it only checked the Node arms.
summary: Under verify-don't-trust-docs rigor the Phase-1 engine's chaos-engineering guarantees hold for every claimed Node-side path. All four system-zone guard sites confirmed live (RedbBackend::put_node line 442, RedbBackend::put_edge line 585, Transaction::put_node_with_attribution transaction.rs:320-328, Transaction::put_edge transaction.rs:387-391). Context-aware paths (put_node_with_context line 811, put_edge_with_context line 605) respect ctx.is_privileged. NodeStore/EdgeStore trait delegates (lines 1087, 1103) route through the guarded inherents, so a trait-object call cannot smuggle a system-zone write. Nested-transaction refusal is doubly-guarded: eager TxGuard::try_acquire on the outer RedbBackend::transaction (line 847) rejects before begin_write_txn; the inner Transaction::transaction (transaction.rs:547-552) rejects before invoking the closure. Panic containment is now three-layered AND pinned by regression tests: benten-graph/src/transaction.rs:588 fan_out catch_unwind, benten-engine/src/change.rs:122 ChangeBroadcast::publish catch_unwind (with test at change.rs:185), benten-ivm/src/subscriber.rs:199 apply_event catch_unwind. Engine::transaction (engine.rs:1543-1625) uses std::sync::Mutex with lock_recover everywhere; the closure-panic path is pinned by engine_transaction_policy_panic_safe.rs:88-120 (same-engine retry succeeds). Change-stream buffer is bounded at CHANGE_STREAM_MAX_BUFFERED = 16_384 (engine.rs:73) with drop-oldest eviction live in record_event (engine.rs:205-208), pinned by change_stream_bounded_under_probe_without_drain (tests/integration/change_stream.rs:71-134). PrimitiveHost buffer-replay atomicity holds for the Node path (engine.rs:968-983 + tx.put_node_with_attribution + replay transaction is the sole commit boundary so ALL-or-NONE is real) BUT the Edge arms (engine.rs:987-992) are a silent no-op: PrimitiveHost::put_edge and delete_edge return Ok to the evaluator AND buffer on pending_ops AND the buffered op is then ignored at replay. Phase-1 dormant (no evaluator primitive calls host.put_edge — verified via exhaustive grep across benten-eval) but a real torn-state shape for any Phase-2 implementer wiring edge-producing primitives. Five new minor findings (one is a shape-upgrade of pass-1 r6-ce-4 now that the O(M) inversion landed; one is the NEW edge-replay drop).

g3-ce-1.verified_line_numbers: crates/benten-graph/src/redb_backend.rs:436-478 (put_node entry + put_node_unchecked), :53-65 (guard_system_zone_node), :1087-1089 (NodeStore delegate). Pass-1 cited lines 434-442 + 448 + 1085 — current lines are 436-444 + 450 + 1087. The guard pattern (entry-level check + fn-private unchecked helper) is exactly as pass-1 described. CONFIRMED.

g3-ce-2.verified_line_numbers: crates/benten-graph/src/redb_backend.rs:584-607 (put_edge + put_edge_with_context + put_edge_unchecked), :72-79 (guard_system_zone_edge), Transaction::put_edge at transaction.rs:386-411 applies the same prefix check in-txn (line 387-391). Pass-1 cited 582-597 + 610 + transaction.rs:333-338 — current lines shift (code moved by the R6 Wave 2 split and subsequent edits) but the semantics are intact. CONFIRMED.

g3-ce-3.verified_line_numbers: crates/benten-engine/src/engine.rs:1543-1625 (Engine::transaction). Uses std::sync::Mutex for both ops_cell (line 1544) and user_result (line 1545). All mutation sites call lock_recover() (via the MutexExt trait — lines 1553, 1581, 1596, 1600, 1608). The terminating let saved = user_result.into_inner().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) at line 1608 recovers from poison. engine_transaction_policy_panic_safe.rs tests land: closure_panic_does_not_wedge_engine_for_subsequent_transactions at lines 88-120 proves SAME-engine retry after a closure panic works (line 111 reuses `engine`). HOWEVER policy_panic_does_not_wedge_engine_for_subsequent_transactions at lines 46-85 uses a DIFFERENT engine (line 72 creates engine2 with a fresh path) — the code CAN survive a policy panic on the same engine (the Mutex recovery path is correct), but the test DOES NOT pin that claim. Pass-1 flagged this as g3-p2-ce-2 still open; reconfirmed. Same-engine-policy-panic is a latent test-coverage gap, not a correctness gap.

g3-ce-4.verified_line_numbers: crates/benten-graph/src/transaction.rs:65-138 (PendingOp enum — DeleteNode variant carries cid + labels: Vec<String> + node: Option<Node> at lines 114-123; DeleteEdge carries cid + label: Option<String> + source: Option<Cid> + target: Option<Cid> at lines 127-137). The read-before-delete in delete_node (lines 428-488) opens a NODES_TABLE read on the SAME WriteTransaction at line 444, captures the decoded Node, then writes the removal — race-free under redb serializable isolation. delete_edge follows the same pattern (lines 501-540). to_change_event (lines 153-219) maps labels and endpoints through to the emitted ChangeEvent. Pass-1 cited transaction.rs:99-122 + 380-397 + 438-458 + 167-196 — all shifted post-split but semantics intact. CONFIRMED.

g3-ce-5.verified: TODO(phase-2-dead-letter) at transaction.rs:568-570. Stderr breadcrumb at fan_out lines 593-597 still the operator-visible signal floor. Matches pass-1.

g3-ce-7.verified: TODO(phase-2) around the per-Arc snapshot-registration-race notes still stands; pass-1 cited redb_backend.rs:197 — same region in current code. Cross-Arc falls through to redb's single-writer lock.

g3-ce-8.verified: tx_id monotonicity-across-reopen still a Phase-2 item; in-process AtomicU64 at redb_backend.rs next_tx_id is unchanged.

g3-ce-9.verified: concurrent_reader_writer_soak.rs + mvcc_snapshot.rs present. Explicit mid-transaction reader-sees-pre-commit assertion still not landed; redb's serializable isolation provides the contract. No regression observed.

g3-ce-10.verified: Subscriber-registration-during-commit ordering docstring present at redb_backend.rs:860-865 (the snapshot-subscribers-AFTER-commit note). IVM author guidance preserved.

g3-ce-11.verified: Policy-panic via code path is sound (Mutex lock_recover on every user_result mutation); subscriber-panic is pinned by subscriber_panic_isolation test. Real commit-time I/O failure injection (disk-full during redb.commit()) remains unpinned. Matches pass-1 disposition.

system_zone_guard_uniformity.verdict: UNIFORM across all six user-reachable Node write paths AND the four Edge write paths

verified_paths.NodeStore::put_node (trait impl): GUARDED @ redb_backend.rs:1087-1089 — delegates to inherent put_node
verified_paths.EdgeStore::put_edge (trait impl): GUARDED @ redb_backend.rs:1103-1105 — delegates to inherent put_edge
verified_paths.PrimitiveHost::put_node (buffered replay: GUARDED @ engine.rs:975 — replay goes through tx.put_node_with_attribution
verified_paths.PrimitiveHost::put_edge (buffered): N/A — replay is a silent no-op; see r6b-ce-2 below for the torn-state hazard

system_zone_guard_uniformity.unchecked_write_path: NONE — put_node_unchecked (line 450) and put_edge_unchecked (line 612) are fn-private, only reachable from the guarded entry points

panic_containment.verdict: THREE-LAYER isolation live, two layers pinned by regression tests

layer_1_benten_graph_fan_out.effect: per-subscriber panic logged to stderr + tx_id; commit thread continues
layer_1_benten_graph_fan_out.test_pin: NOT pinned explicitly; relies on ChangeBroadcast coverage at change.rs:185

layer_2_benten_engine_change_broadcast.effect: one misbehaving callback cannot poison the broadcast; other callbacks still fire
layer_2_benten_engine_change_broadcast.test_pin: change.rs:184-195 panicking_callback_does_not_poison_broadcast asserts the sibling callback still runs and the counter advances

layer_3_benten_ivm_apply_event.effect: panicked view marks itself stale; other views continue to receive the event

engine_transaction_closure_panic.recovery_mechanism: std::sync::Mutex everywhere + MutexExt::lock_recover (unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())) at every lock site — line 1553, 1581, 1596, 1600; user_result.into_inner().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) at line 1608
engine_transaction_closure_panic.test_pin_closure_panic: engine_transaction_policy_panic_safe.rs:88-120 — SAME engine retry after closure panic succeeds
engine_transaction_closure_panic.test_pin_policy_panic_same_engine: MISSING — policy_panic test (lines 46-85) uses engine2 built with a fresh DB path. The code path IS correct (Mutex poison-recovery is exercised), but no regression test exercises `engine.transaction(...) → panic → engine.transaction(...) retry` specifically for a policy panic. Latent pass-1 r6-ce- concern; declared 'sound by construction' but not test-pinned.

change_stream_bound.verdict: LIVE at 16_384 default; operator-tunable; drop-oldest eviction active
change_stream_bound.enforcement_site: EngineInner::record_event @ engine.rs:195-211 — while guard.len() >= self.change_stream_capacity { guard.remove(0); dropped_events.fetch_add(1, SeqCst); }
change_stream_bound.tap_wiring: builder.rs:237-242 — broadcast.subscribe_fn(move |event| inner_for_tap.record_event(event)); the tap is the ONLY producer to observed_events, so every ChangeEvent routes through the bounded path
change_stream_bound.builder_override: EngineBuilder::change_stream_capacity @ builder.rs:233 unwraps to the default when unset
change_stream_bound.regression_test: crates/benten-engine/tests/integration/change_stream.rs:70-134 (change_stream_bounded_under_probe_without_drain) — writes 3x capacity without draining, asserts dropped_events metric == overflow count, asserts post-saturation write still succeeds
change_stream_bound.bypass_audit: grep for observed_events.push shows ONE site (record_event); no direct .push/.insert bypasses the bounded-drain check. CONFIRMED UNIFORM.

nested_transaction_refusal.verdict: LIVE on both outer and inner paths; eager rejection before any redb state mutation

outer_rebntrancy_guard.mechanism: tx_flag: Arc<Mutex<bool>> — try_acquire returns NestedTransactionNotSupported before begin_write_txn, so no redb WriteTransaction is opened. Drop releases the flag even on panic.
outer_rebntrancy_guard.guard_raii: crates/benten-graph/src/transaction.rs:227-248 — Drop impl on TxGuard clears the flag via lock_recover

inner_rejection.mechanism: eager Err(GraphError::NestedTransactionNotSupported {}) WITHOUT invoking the closure; no side effects possible before the rejection

nested_transaction_refusal.interaction_with_engine_call_inside_user: An engine.transaction(...) user closure that calls engine.call(...) triggers dispatch_call, whose replay phase calls self.transaction internally. The outer tx_flag is set, so the inner attempt returns NestedTransactionNotSupported, the replay returns EngineError::NestedTransactionNotSupported to dispatch_call's caller, and the outer user closure sees the error. No partial backend state possible — the inner replay never opened a redb txn. Not torn-state; but unobvious operator behavior (see r6b-ce-5 below).

primitive_host_buffer_replay_atomicity.verdict: ALL-or-NONE holds ONLY for the Node path (put_node, delete_node). Edge path is a silent no-op (r6b-ce-2) — the evaluator-visible return value says 'written' but the replay drops the op on the floor.

node_path_verification.put_node: primitive_host.rs:139-177 — buffers PendingHostOp::PutNode on the active_call frame if dispatch is active, else direct backend.put_node(node). Replay at engine.rs:968-983 — uses tx.put_node_with_attribution inside the self.transaction(|tx| ...) closure. Any one op failing with ? returns Err from the closure → TxAborted → redb drops the whole WriteTransaction without commit. ALL-or-NONE confirmed.
node_path_verification.delete_node: primitive_host.rs:194-206 — same buffering shape. Replay at engine.rs:984-986 — tx.delete_node(cid)?. ALL-or-NONE confirmed.
node_path_verification.projected_cid_contract: node.cid() computed at buffer time (line 143), reused in replay (precomputed_cid = Some(projected_cid.clone()) at line 977 → transaction.rs:332-335 skips recomputation). Content-addressed hashing is deterministic so projected == committed CID. Confirmed.

edge_path_VIOLATION.put_edge_buffer: primitive_host.rs:179-192 — buffers PendingHostOp::PutEdge on the active_call frame. RETURNS Ok(projected_cid) to the evaluator, signalling successful write.
edge_path_VIOLATION.delete_edge_buffer: primitive_host.rs:208-220 — buffers PendingHostOp::DeleteEdge. RETURNS Ok(()) to the evaluator.
edge_path_VIOLATION.replay_arm: engine.rs:987-992 — the PutEdge | DeleteEdge match arm is a no-op with a 'Phase-1: edge ops via PrimitiveHost are not surfaced by any test subgraph. Reserved for Phase-2 ...' comment
edge_path_VIOLATION.torn_state_shape: The evaluator sees success → subsequent evaluator steps assume the edge was written → downstream READ or visibility checks would observe absence. Because no Phase-1 evaluator primitive calls host.put_edge (verified by exhaustive grep across benten-eval/src — zero hits for host.put_edge / host.delete_edge), the hazard is DORMANT. It is not a Phase-1 correctness bug. BUT it is a latent Phase-2 footgun: the pass-1 chaos-engineer claim 'buffer-replay path introduces no torn-state hazards' is technically false for this half of the API, and the primitive_host.rs module docstring (lines 7-35) claims 'Rollback-on-error parity. If the evaluator returns Err mid-walk... the pending_ops list is dropped without a commit' which is TRUE but masks the orthogonal bug that the replay for the edge arms is a NO-OP even on the success path.
edge_path_VIOLATION.severity_justification: Minor because dormant + doc-masked; elevated from pass-1 invisible to visible because it contradicts the atomicity claim the pass-1 verdict rested on.

evaluator_panic_mid_walk.mechanism: panic propagates through eval_result match (engine.rs:924); the manual pop at line 866 is SKIPPED (ActiveCall frame leaks on Mutex). Pass-1 r6-ce-2 stays valid. The pending_ops on the leaked frame are never replayed — ALL-or-NONE holds because the replay transaction never opens.
evaluator_panic_mid_walk.unbounded_leak: subsequent dispatch_calls push their own frames on top; last_mut() targets the topmost (correct), so later writes go to the correct per-call frame. BUT the Vec grows unbounded across panics. Still open.

grant_backed_policy_toctou.verdict: UNCHANGED from pass-1 — acceptable by compromise #2 Option A
grant_backed_policy_toctou.location: crates/benten-engine/src/builder.rs:316-403 (BackendGrantReader) — moved from lib.rs:1905-1949 by the R6 Wave 2 split
grant_backed_policy_toctou.window_shape: revoked_scopes() opens 1 get_by_label txn + N per-revocation get_node txns (lines 330-354). has_unrevoked_grant_for_scope opens another get_by_label + M per-grant get_node txns (lines 364-400). Total 2 + N + M separate read snapshots. Between the revoked_scopes() completion and the grant scan, an engine-privileged writer could commit a system:CapabilityRevocation whose scope matches — the grant would pass the scan even though the revocation is now committed.
mitigations_intact: Only engine-privileged paths can write system:-prefixed nodes (system-zone guard on all user paths verified above)
Compromise #2 Option A explicitly accepts 'revocation at iteration N commits at iteration N+1' semantics — documented in docs/SECURITY-POSTURE.md
Each individual get_by_label / get_node is linearizable under redb's serializable isolation — partial reads are impossible
grant_backed_policy_toctou.pass_1_r6_ce_1_status: still open at minor severity; no new evidence to change the disposition

new_findings.claim: ActiveCall frame leak on panic during dispatch_call_inner — pass-1 r6-ce-2 restated with current line numbers. Manual push (lines 849-858) + pop (lines 863-867) has no RAII guard; a panic between pushes skips the pop and leaks the frame onto self.active_call.
new_findings.detail: Line 860 invokes dispatch_call_inner; if it panics, the pop at line 865-866 is unreachable. The Mutex guard is released by Drop, but the frame itself stays on the Vec. Subsequent dispatch_call invocations push their own frames; last_mut returns the topmost (correct), so writes go to the correct frame — but the Vec grows unbounded across panics. Phase-1 workloads do not realistically panic mid-dispatch (no SANDBOX, no user-extensible primitives, evaluator primitives are deterministic), so the latency is negligible.
new_findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a small RAII guard struct CallFrame { engine: &Engine } that pushes on new() and pops in Drop. Mirrors TxGuard. Two-line change + one regression test injecting a panic from a test-only PrimitiveHost impl and asserting active_call.lock().is_empty() post-catch_unwind.

new_findings.claim: PrimitiveHost::put_edge and PrimitiveHost::delete_edge buffer onto ActiveCall.pending_ops AND return Ok to the evaluator, but the replay arm silently drops the op. This contradicts the 'buffer+replay ALL-or-NONE' atomicity claim for the edge half of the API.
new_findings.detail: primitive_host.rs:179-192 buffers PendingHostOp::PutEdge { edge, projected_cid } and returns Ok(projected); primitive_host.rs:208-220 buffers PendingHostOp::DeleteEdge { cid } and returns Ok(()). At engine.rs:987-992 the match arm `PendingHostOp::PutEdge { .. } | PendingHostOp::DeleteEdge { .. } => { /* Phase-1: not surfaced */ }` is a no-op: the buffered op is dropped without hitting the transaction. Evaluator-side, the StepResult for a WRITE primitive targeting an edge would echo the projected CID as if the edge landed. Dormant in Phase-1: exhaustive grep across crates/benten-eval/src for `host.put_edge` / `host.delete_edge` / `PrimitiveHost::put_edge` returns zero call sites — no evaluator primitive currently writes edges through the host. The hazard is 100% Phase-2 where any edge-producing evaluator primitive (e.g., a BIND primitive for the capability layer) would trust the evaluator's Ok and corrupt downstream semantic assumptions. The primitive_host.rs module docstring claims 'Rollback-on-error parity' for the buffered path; that phrasing is true (Err does not write) but does not flag that the SUCCESS path also silently drops edges. Pass-1 chaos-engineer did not surface this because it only checked the Node arms.
new_findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) make PrimitiveHost::put_edge and delete_edge return EvalError::Unsupported("edge writes via PrimitiveHost are Phase-2") right now so the evaluator can never observe the torn state, OR (b) wire the replay arm through tx.put_edge / tx.delete_edge (a five-line change — tx.put_edge is already the privileged-aware entry point). Option (a) is safer Phase-1 because it fails loud; option (b) needs an end-to-end test with a real edge-producing primitive which is Phase-2 scope anyway. Recommend option (a) immediately, land option (b) when edge-producing primitives are added.

new_findings.claim: Pass-1 r6-ce-3 restated — emit_event being a Phase-1 no-op is correct for Phase-1 but masks a Phase-2 torn-state hazard. An implementer wiring emit_event to the ChangeBroadcast fan-out will leak events for a dispatch_call that subsequently fails at replay (capability denial, system-zone reject). The docstring at lines 249-252 notes 'Reserved for Phase-2' but does not flag the torn-state hazard.
new_findings.detail: User subgraphs can include EMIT primitives today — the evaluator accepts them and calls PrimitiveHost::emit_event during the walk, which is BEFORE the replay transaction commits. A Phase-2 implementer who wires emit_event to the ChangeBroadcast publish path (the obvious next step for reactive subscribers) will have external observers see EMITted events for calls that subsequently fail at replay. Same shape as the edge-replay hazard but worse because it's observable to external subscribers, not just downstream evaluator steps.
new_findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line doc-note warning at primitive_host.rs:248 and on the PrimitiveHost::emit_event trait declaration (host.rs in benten-eval): 'Phase-2 MUST buffer emits into the ActiveCall frame and fire only after successful transaction commit. Firing inline creates a torn-state hazard when the replay transaction fails.' Zero runtime cost, prevents a real Phase-2 regression.

new_findings.claim: Pass-1 r6-ce-4 is PARTIALLY ADDRESSED — the r6-perf-4 inversion at line 580 lifts event construction out of the per-subscriber loop, so now ChangeEvent is constructed ONCE per op. A per-subscriber ChangeEvent::clone still happens at line 584. With N subscribers + M ops + large Node bodies the memory shape is now O(M) construction + O(N*M) clones, down from the pass-1 O(N*M) construction + O(N*M) clones. The Arc<ChangeEvent> phase-2 refactor pass-1 suggested would drop the clones to cheap Arc-bumps.
new_findings.detail: Pass-1 claim was: 'fan_out clones the full ChangeEvent (including Option<Node>) per subscriber per op, scaling memory as O(N_subs * M_ops * payload_size).' Current code (line 580 `let events: Vec<ChangeEvent> = ops.iter().map(|op| op.to_change_event(tx_id)).collect();`) builds each event ONCE, then the inner loop at 581-599 clones the event per subscriber at line 584 (`let event_clone = event.clone();`) to move into the catch_unwind closure. The clone is still there because the closure needs owned data; an Arc wrapper would skip the expensive ChangeEvent::clone (which clones the Option<Node> body) in favor of an Arc::clone. Phase-1 has at most 1 subscriber (IVM) so the hit is invisible; flagging as informational because the partial fix moved the needle without closing the Phase-2 concern.
new_findings.suggested_fix: Phase-2 only: change the fan_out signature to accept Vec<Arc<ChangeEvent>> (or build the Arc list inline at line 580), then Arc::clone inside the catch_unwind closure. Bench delta at 5 subscribers + 100-op batch + 100KB Node body to confirm the win.

new_findings.claim: engine.transaction(|tx| engine.call(handler, op, ...)) surfaces an opaque EngineError::NestedTransactionNotSupported because dispatch_call's buffer-replay phase tries to open its own transaction, which collides with the outer user transaction. No torn state, but the error message does not name the cause.
new_findings.detail: Trace: user calls engine.transaction(|tx| { engine.call('h', 'op', node); Ok(()) }). Outer tx acquires tx_flag (RedbBackend::transaction line 847). Inside the closure, engine.call invokes dispatch_call → pushes ActiveCall frame → runs Evaluator → buffers pending ops on the frame. Replay phase at engine.rs:958-997 calls self.transaction(|tx| ...) — the INNER RedbBackend::transaction hits TxGuard::try_acquire, which sees tx_flag=true and returns NestedTransactionNotSupported. The inner error bubbles up as EngineError::NestedTransactionNotSupported. The outer user closure returns Err, the outer tx aborts, nothing lands. No partial state — but the user sees 'nested transactions are not supported' without any hint that the cause was an internal buffer-replay. For Phase-1 this is probably rare enough to ignore (users who write engine.transaction(...) blocks typically use the tx-scoped create_node/create_edge surface rather than engine.call), but the error is confusing.
new_findings.suggested_fix: Option A: document at Engine::transaction that 'engine.call is not supported inside the closure; use tx.create_node / tx.create_edge directly'. Option B: detect the nesting inside dispatch_call (check if the active_call Vec is non-empty AND the backend tx_flag is set; rare but signals the user-transaction path) and return a clearer EngineError::CallNotAllowedInsideTransaction. Phase-1: option A (doc-only) since this is not a torn-state hazard.

counts_by_severity.note: r6b-ce-4 is reclassified informational (partial-fix already landed); r6b-ce-2 is a newly-visible hazard that pass-1 missed; r6b-ce-1/3/5 are pass-1 restatements with verified line numbers.

pass_1_findings_dispositions_still_valid.r6-ce-1_grant_toctou: still valid — bounded by compromise #2 Option A; no code change; line numbers moved to builder.rs:316-403
pass_1_findings_dispositions_still_valid.r6-ce-2_active_call_leak: still valid — restated as r6b-ce-1 with current line numbers
pass_1_findings_dispositions_still_valid.r6-ce-3_emit_event_phase2_hazard: still valid — restated as r6b-ce-3; doc note has NOT landed
pass_1_findings_dispositions_still_valid.r6-ce-4_fan_out_clone_scaling: partially addressed — r6-perf-4 inversion cuts half the cost; Arc wrap remains Phase-2. Reclassified informational in r6b.
pass_1_findings_dispositions_still_valid.r6-ce-5_100MB_bench_opaque_oom: not re-examined in r6b (out of scope for the re-verification prompt); assumed unchanged from pass-1

new_findings_most_important: r6b-ce-2 (NEW) — PrimitiveHost edge-replay silent drop contradicts the atomicity claim pass-1 endorsed. Phase-1 dormant, but a real torn-state shape the pass-1 analysis missed because it only audited the Node arms. Flag-don't-re-spin disposition: recommend making put_edge/delete_edge fail loud (EvalError::Unsupported) immediately to prevent Phase-2 regressions.
untested_scenarios_most_dangerous_phase1: Same-engine retry after a POLICY panic (not a closure panic) — the code path is correct but engine_transaction_policy_panic_safe.rs:46-85 uses a different engine. One added test would close the latent gap.
ActiveCall frame leak on mid-dispatch panic (r6b-ce-1) — zero test coverage.
PrimitiveHost::put_edge / delete_edge torn-state (r6b-ce-2) — zero test coverage because no Phase-1 evaluator calls the edge primitives via the host.
engine.transaction(|tx| engine.call(...)) nested-transaction surprise (r6b-ce-5) — no test pins the error surface.
notes: Under the verify-don't-trust-docs methodology the pass-1 PASS-WITH-MINORS verdict holds. The refactor since pass-1 (R6 Wave 2 split of lib.rs into 11 modules, plus the r6-perf-4 fan_out inversion, plus the 16_384 change-stream bound, plus the cap-refresh live code) is MATERIALLY BETTER than pass-1 state — the bounded change stream is live and tested, the three-layer panic containment is confirmed in code AND pinned by tests, the Node-side buffer-replay atomicity is genuinely ALL-or-NONE. The new finding r6b-ce-2 is a documentation/contract gap (the atomicity claim was slightly broader than the code supports, for a Phase-1 dormant surface), not a shipping bug. Recommend: file r6b-ce-2 on the R4b docket with a preference for the fail-loud mitigation since it is cheap and prevents a Phase-2 regression. Do not re-spin Phase 1.
